channel.title: BeatTheJob Blog
channel.description: Practical resume advice backed by how hiring actually works. Learn what ATS systems scan for, which keywords matter, and how to stand out in 7 seconds.

item.title: Effective Resume Design Tips for Job Seekers
item.description: Design tips to make your resume stand out and get noticed by employers without breaking ATS parsing.

item.title: Why Sending the Same Resume to Every Job is Costing You Interviews
item.description: 75% of resumes get filtered by ATS before a human sees them. Learn why sending generic resumes costs you interviews and how to fix it.

item.title: What Recruiters Actually Look for in the First 7 Seconds
item.description: Recruiters spend about 7 seconds on your resume before deciding. Here is exactly what they look at and how to make those seconds count.

item.title: How to Explain Resume Gaps Without Hurting Your Chances
item.description: Resume gaps are more common than ever. Here is how to address them honestly without scaring off hiring managers.

item.title: How Often Should You Update Your Resume
item.description: Most people only update their resume when they need a job. Here is why thats a mistake and how often you should actually do it.

item.title: Should You Include Your GPA on Your Resume
item.description: Including your GPA on your resume depends on a few factors. Here is when it helps, when it hurts, and when it doesnt matter.

item.title: How to Tailor Your Resume for Remote Job Applications
item.description: Remote jobs get hundreds of applications. Here is how to tailor your resume to stand out in a remote-first hiring process.

item.title: 50 Action Verbs That Make Your Resume Stand Out
item.description: Stop starting every bullet point with Managed or Responsible for. Here are 50 action verbs that make your resume stronger.

item.title: How to Write a Professional Summary That Gets Read
item.description: Your professional summary is the first thing recruiters read. Here is how to write one that makes them keep going.

item.title: How to Write a Resume With No Work Experience
item.description: No work experience doesnt mean an empty resume. Here is how to build a strong resume using what you already have.

item.title: How to Write a Resume When You Are Changing Careers
item.description: Switching careers doesnt mean starting from scratch. Here is how to write a resume that connects your old experience to your new direction.

item.title: How to Pass ATS Screening and Get Your Resume to a Human
item.description: Most resumes get rejected by ATS software before a human ever sees them. Here is how to make sure yours gets through.

item.title: 5 Resume Mistakes That Make Hiring Managers Skip Yours
item.description: These common resume mistakes cost people interviews every day. Most dont know they are making them.

item.title: One Page or Two Page Resume? Here is How to Decide
item.description: The real answer to the one page vs two page resume debate. It depends on your experience, not a universal rule.

item.title: The Resume Keywords That Actually Get You Interviews
item.description: How to find the right keywords in any job posting and place them on your resume for maximum ATS match score.

item.title: How ATS Systems Actually Work (And Why Your Resume Gets Rejected)
item.description: What Workday, Greenhouse, and Lever actually do with your resume. Learn how ATS filtering works and how to pass it.
